title: Weekly Calendar Management Planning
user_persona: Engineering Manager Marcus Johnson at Vertex Labs: Slack is the primary channel for team coordination and notifications; Telegram is used for personal reminders and deadline alerts; Gmail is used for client communication and self-archiving weekly summaries.

edges.relation: Architecture review conducted after Sprint planning
PRD must be completed within the Sprint week

signal_calendar_events.description:
Sprint 24 Planning Session

Agenda:
1. Review and prioritize backlog items from Product team
2. Assign user stories to team members based on capacity
3. Estimate story points using planning poker
4. Identify technical dependencies and blockers
5. Define sprint goal and success metrics

Pre-read Requirements:
- Project Phoenix PRD (latest version in Google Drive)
- Technical debt backlog from Sprint 23 retrospective
- QA regression test results from last release

Capacity Notes:
- Alice Wong: 80% capacity (1 day PTO on Thursday)
- Bob Kumar: 100% capacity
- Chen Mei: 60% capacity (supporting production incident)

Definition of Done:
- Code reviewed and merged
- Unit tests passing (>90% coverage)
- Integration tests passing
- Documentation updated
- Demo-ready for sprint review

Product Demo for Acme Corp Stakeholders

Objective: Demonstrate v2.1 release features to Acme Corp executive team and gather feedback for Q2 roadmap alignment.

Demo Agenda:
1. (10 min) Overview of v2.1 new features
2. (20 min) Live demo of dashboard analytics module
3. (15 min) API integration capabilities walkthrough
4. (15 min) Q&A and feedback collection

Required Preparation:
- Slide deck: Use the Q1 product deck template, update with v2.1 screenshots
- Live environment: Stage environment must be seeded with Acme Corp sample data
- Backup recording setup: Start Zoom recording as backup in case client needs to share internally

Key Stakeholders from Acme Corp:
- James Taylor (VP Engineering) — primary decision maker
- Priya Sharma (Technical Architect) — evaluating technical fit

Follow-up: Send meeting notes within 24 hours, include action items and next steps.

Bi-weekly 1:1 Meeting with Alice Wong

Agenda:
1. Career growth discussion — review progress on tech lead track
2. Q1 OKR check-in — are we on track for the 3 key results?
3. Feedback on recent code review process changes
4. Any blockers or concerns?

Notes from last 1:1:
- Alice expressed interest in leading the microservices migration project
- Discussed timeline for senior engineer promotion (target: Q3 2026)
- Action item: Alice to prepare architecture proposal by end of month

Architecture Review: Monolith to Microservices Migration

RFC Document: https://docs.google.com/document/d/abc123

Key Decision Points:
1. Service boundary definitions — where to split the monolith
2. Data ownership model — which service owns which data?
3. Inter-service communication pattern (REST vs gRPC vs event-driven)
4. Migration strategy (strangler fig vs big bang)

Required Attendees:
- Marcus Johnson (Engineering Manager) — decision authority
- Alice Wong (Senior Engineer) — architecture proposal author
- Bob Kumar (Backend Lead) — implementation lead
- Chen Mei (DevOps) — infrastructure planning

Pre-read:
- RFC document (link above)
- Current monolith dependency graph
- AWS cost projection for microservices architecture

Decision: Service boundaries and data ownership MUST be finalized in this meeting to unblock Sprint 25 planning.

Daily team sync.
Format: Each person shares blockers, yesterday's progress, today's plan.
Keep it under 15 minutes.

Daily team sync.
Format: Each person shares blockers, yesterday's progress, today's plan.
Keep it under 15 minutes.

Daily team sync.
Format: Each person shares blockers, yesterday's progress, today's plan.
Keep it under 15 minutes.

signal_calendar_events.summary: DEADLINE: Project Phoenix PRD Final Review
signal_calendar_events.description:
DEADLINE: The final PRD for Project Phoenix must be approved by end of day.

Required Actions:
- All stakeholders must sign off on requirements
- Engineering team confirms technical feasibility
- Legal review of data handling section complete
- Final version uploaded to Google Drive shared folder

Stakeholder sign-off needed from: VP Product, VP Engineering, Legal Counsel.

qa_annotations.question: What's on my calendar this week (Feb 18-20)? Give me a day-by-day overview.
qa_annotations.answer: 8 calendar events total. Wednesday 2/18: Daily Standup 09:00-09:15, Sprint 24 Planning 10:00-11:30. Thursday 2/19: Daily Standup 09:00-09:15, Acme Corp Product Demo 14:00-15:00, 1:1 with Alice Wong 15:00-15:30. Friday 2/20: Daily Standup 09:00-09:15, Architecture Review 09:30-11:00, plus all-day DEADLINE: Project Phoenix PRD Final Review. Note: 1:1 with Alice was rescheduled from Wed to Thu earlier in the session.
qa_annotations.ground_truth: 8 calendar events total. Wednesday 2/18: Daily Standup 09:00-09:15, Sprint 24 Planning 10:00-11:30. Thursday 2/19: Daily Standup 09:00-09:15, Acme Corp Product Demo 14:00-15:00, 1:1 with Alice Wong 15:00-15:30. Friday 2/20: Daily Standup 09:00-09:15, Architecture Review 09:30-11:00, plus all-day DEADLINE: Project Phoenix PRD Final Review. The 1:1 with Alice was rescheduled from Wed 2/18 to Thu 2/19 earlier in the conversation.

scoring_rubric.criterion: Wed 2/18 events correct (Standup, Sprint Planning — 1:1 was rescheduled off Wed)
Thu 2/19 events correct (Standup, Acme Demo, 1:1 with Alice)
Fri 2/20 events correct (Standup, Arch Review, PRD deadline)

All 3 exist as Google Tasks (created earlier in session or created now).
qa_annotations.ground_truth: 3 items: Slide deck, Live demo environment, Backup recording setup. All 3 should be in Google Tasks (tasks were created earlier in the session; agent may confirm they exist rather than re-create).

qa_annotations.question: What meeting comes right after the daily standup on Friday 2/20? Send the Architecture Review agenda to the team on Slack.
qa_annotations.answer: After the Friday standup (09:00-09:15), the next meeting is Architecture Review - Microservices Migration (09:30-11:00, Conference Room A). 15-minute buffer between them. Agenda: 1) Service boundary definitions, 2) Data ownership model, 3) Inter-service communication pattern (REST vs gRPC vs event-driven), 4) Migration strategy (strangler fig vs big bang). Sent to Slack.
qa_annotations.ground_truth: Architecture Review - Microservices Migration, 09:30-11:00. Agenda covers service boundaries, data ownership, communication patterns, migration strategy. Sent to Slack.

scoring_rubric.criterion: Correctly identified Architecture Review as next meeting
Agenda content correct (service boundaries, data ownership)

qa_annotations.question: I want to move the 1:1 with Alice to Thursday 2/19 at 15:00-15:30. Any conflicts? If not, please reschedule it.
qa_annotations.answer: The 1:1 with Alice was already rescheduled to Thursday 2/19 15:00-15:30 earlier in the session. No conflict — Demo ends at 15:00, so the slot is free (0 overlap but no buffer). Agent correctly identifies the event is already at the target time.
qa_annotations.ground_truth: No conflict on 2/19 15:00-15:30. Demo ends at 15:00. The 1:1 was already moved to this slot earlier in the conversation; agent should recognize it is already there or confirm the update.

qa_annotations.answer: Yes, one deadline: DEADLINE: Project Phoenix PRD Final Review on Friday 2/20 (all-day). Sign-off needed from VP Product, VP Engineering, Legal Counsel. Reminder sent via Telegram.

qa_annotations.answer: No, after rescheduling the 1:1 to Thursday, Wednesday 2/18 has only 2 meetings: Daily Standup 15 minutes (09:00-09:15), Sprint 24 Planning 90 minutes (10:00-11:30). Total: 105 minutes. The 1:1 with Alice (30 min) was moved to Thursday 2/19.
qa_annotations.ground_truth: After reschedule, Feb 18 has 2 meetings: Standup 15 min + Sprint Planning 90 min = 105 minutes. 1:1 moved to Feb 19.

scoring_rubric.criterion: Correctly identifies only 2 meetings on Feb 18 after reschedule

Google Tasks: 3 demo prep items (slide deck, demo environment, backup recording). Summary sent to Slack and archived via Gmail to zihangzhou26@gmail.com.

qa_annotations.question: Who on the team has reduced capacity this sprint? What are the specifics?
qa_annotations.answer: Two team members have reduced capacity: 1) Alice Wong at 80% capacity — 1 day PTO on Thursday. 2) Chen Mei at 60% capacity — supporting a production incident. Bob Kumar is at 100% capacity.

qa_annotations.question: What are the 4 key decision points for the Architecture Review meeting?
qa_annotations.answer: The 4 key decision points: 1) Service boundary definitions — where to split the monolith, 2) Data ownership model — which service owns which data, 3) Inter-service communication pattern (REST vs gRPC vs event-driven), 4) Migration strategy (strangler fig vs big bang). Service boundaries and data ownership MUST be finalized to unblock Sprint 25 planning.
qa_annotations.ground_truth: 1) Service boundary definitions, 2) Data ownership model, 3) Inter-service communication (REST/gRPC/event-driven), 4) Migration strategy (strangler fig/big bang).

qa_annotations.answer: The 1:1 with Alice was rescheduled to Thursday 2/19, 15:00-15:30. The previous meeting on Thursday is the Acme Corp Demo ending at 15:00. There is 0-minute gap (demo ends exactly when 1:1 starts), which does NOT meet the preferred 15-minute buffer between meetings.

scoring_rubric.criterion: Correctly recalls 1:1 is now on Thursday 2/19

qa_annotations.answer: The 1:1 with Alice was rescheduled to Thursday 2/19, so it is no longer on Wednesday 2/18. After Sprint Planning ends at 11:30, there are no more meetings on Feb 18.
qa_annotations.ground_truth: 1:1 was rescheduled to Feb 19. Sprint Planning ends 11:30. No 1:1 on Feb 18 anymore.

qa_annotations.answer: No, the Architecture Review is on Friday 2/20 (09:30-11:00), not Thursday. Thursday 2/19 has the Daily Standup (09:00-09:15) and the Acme Corp Product Demo (14:00-15:00). The Architecture Review is in Conference Room A on Friday morning, right after the Friday standup.
qa_annotations.ground_truth: No, Architecture Review is on Friday 2/20, not Thursday. Thursday has Standup + Acme Demo only.

qa_annotations.question: What's the Definition of Done for Sprint 24? And who needs to sign off on the PRD deadline?
qa_annotations.answer: Definition of Done: 1) Code reviewed and merged, 2) Unit tests passing (>90% coverage), 3) Integration tests passing, 4) Documentation updated, 5) Demo-ready for sprint review. PRD sign-off needed from: VP Product, VP Engineering, and Legal Counsel.

event_summary_ground_truth: There are 8 calendar events for 2/18-2/20. After rescheduling the 1:1 with Alice from Wed to Thu, Wednesday 2/18 has 2 meetings (Standup 15 min, Sprint Planning 90 min, totaling 105 minutes). Thursday 2/19 has a standup, Acme Corp Product Demo (14:00-15:00, Google Meet, attendees james.taylor and priya.sharma, needs preparation of slide deck, demo environment, and backup recording), and the rescheduled 1:1 with Alice Wong (15:00-15:30, 0 buffer after demo). Friday 2/20 has a standup and Architecture Review (09:30-11:00, agenda is service boundary and data ownership decisions for microservices migration), as well as the PRD Final Review all-day deadline (requires stakeholder sign-off before EOD).
user_preferences: Calendar overview preferred to be displayed grouped by day
Keep at least a 15-minute buffer between meetings
Work summary simultaneously archived to Gmail
